title: Out-of-fold empirical hazard priors by strategy state
summary: The strongest current features encode physics-like windows but not learned historical stop propensity for specific race/lap/stint/compound states.
rationale: The strongest current features encode physics-like windows but not learned historical stop propensity for specific race/lap/stint/compound states. Leakage-safe target encoding can convert sparse categorical strategy states into smooth empirical hazards while avoiding high-cardinality overfit.
implementation_hint: Create bins for RaceProgress, TyreLife, LapsRemaining_Est, Position, and degradation. In grouped OOF folds, compute smoothed PitNextLap means for combinations such as Race_Year x Compound x StintBin, Race x LapBin, Compound x TyreLifeBin, and TestingFlag x LapBin. Add these priors as numeric features, then refit mappings on full train for test.
expected_effect: May add a low-variance prior that captures circuit/year strategy templates missed by hand thresholds, with less model dependence than a full CatBoost CTR sidecar.
risk: Target leakage is the main failure mode; encodings must be built inside folds and validation should group by Race_Year or use a time-aware split.
